---
title: "Formats"
---

livefigures supports eighteen figure source formats through one pipeline.
The unifying idea: each is a **plain-text or JSON notation with real
authoring communities and — critically — high LLM fluency**, so both
humans and AI agents can create, review, and diff figure sources the same
way they handle code. Formats marked *kroki* render through a
[kroki](https://kroki.io/) endpoint (network on cache misses only,
self-hostable); everything else renders locally and offline.

## Sketching & general diagrams

| Format | Extensions | Rendering | Covers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Excalidraw](https://excalidraw.com/) | `.excalidraw` | local | Freehand whiteboard sketches: architecture drawings, annotated mockups, anything you'd draw for a colleague. The scene file is JSON; the editor is free and browser-based. |
| [D2](https://d2lang.com/) | `.d2` | kroki | A modern declarative diagram language with clean syntax and automatic layout — a good default for boxes-and-arrows when you aren't sketching by hand. |
| [ditaa](https://ditaa.sourceforge.net/) | `.ditaa` | kroki | Turns ASCII-art boxes into polished raster-style diagrams; useful when the source must remain readable as plain text. |
| [svgbob](https://github.com/ivanceras/svgbob) | `.svgbob` | kroki | ASCII art to faithful SVG, preserving the hand-typed aesthetic. |
| [pikchr](https://pikchr.org/) | `.pikchr` | kroki | The modern descendant of Kernighan's PIC language [@kernighan1984pic], used throughout SQLite and Fossil documentation. |
| [TikZ](https://tikz.dev/) | `.tikz` | kroki | Publication-grade LaTeX graphics. Sources must be complete `standalone` documents. |

## Charts & data graphics

| Format | Extensions | Rendering | Covers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Vega-Lite](https://vega.github.io/vega-lite/) | `.vl.json` | local | A grammar of interactive graphics [@satyanarayan2017vegalite], descended from Wilkinson's *Grammar of Graphics* [@wilkinson2005grammar]. The most agent-fluent chart format there is: concise, declarative JSON. |
| [Vega](https://vega.github.io/vega/) | `.vg.json` | local | The lower-level dataflow language beneath Vega-Lite [@satyanarayan2016reactive]. Reaches things Vega-Lite can't, including force-directed network layouts [@fruchterman1991graph]. |

## Graphs, networks & software structure

| Format | Extensions | Rendering | Covers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Graphviz](https://graphviz.org/) | `.dot`, `.gv` | local | The canonical graph-layout system [@gansner2000graphviz]. Dependency graphs, state machines, pipelines — `dot` has four decades of mindshare and correspondingly deep LLM fluency. |
| [nomnoml](https://nomnoml.com/) | `.noml`, `.nomnoml` | local | UML-ish boxes and arrows from remarkably terse text, laid out hierarchically in the Sugiyama style [@sugiyama1981methods]. The fastest path from idea to node-edge diagram. |
| [PlantUML](https://plantuml.com/) | `.puml`, `.plantuml` | kroki | The workhorse of software UML: sequence, class, activity, state, component diagrams and more. |
| [C4-PlantUML](https://github.com/plantuml-stdlib/C4-PlantUML) | `.c4` | kroki | C4-model architecture diagrams (context/container/component) in PlantUML syntax. |
| [Structurizr](https://structurizr.com/) | `.structurizr` | kroki | C4 architecture from the dedicated Structurizr DSL — model once, view many. |

## Data modeling

| Format | Extensions | Rendering | Covers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [DBML](https://dbml.dbdiagram.io/) | `.dbml` | local | Database schemas — tables, columns, relationships — in the popular DBML language. |
| [erd](https://github.com/BurntSushi/erd) | `.erd` | kroki | Compact entity-relationship diagrams in a minimal text notation. |

## Hardware & protocol

| Format | Extensions | Rendering | Covers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [WaveDrom](https://wavedrom.com/) | `.wavedrom`, `.wavedrom.json` | local | Digital timing diagrams and register/bit-field layouts from JSON — the de-facto standard in hardware documentation. |
| [bytefield](https://bytefield-svg.deepsymmetry.org/) | `.bytefield` | local | Byte- and packet-layout diagrams, in the tradition of RFC header figures. |

## What we deliberately left out

Selection is documented in the project's
[ADRs](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/tree/main/docs/adr):
formats requiring hand-placed coordinates (UMLet, BPMN-DI) fail the
agent-authorability test; tldraw's SDK license prohibits open-source
redistribution; the blockdiag family is broken on public kroki; Mermaid
and code-cell Graphviz are already native to Quarto. SMILES (chemistry)
and ABC notation (music) are queued next.
